sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with anxiety about recent blood work results showing hepatitis C, despite feeling fine and no prior knowledge of the condition.
She reported a history of IV drug use and alcohol use, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She currently smokes one to two cigarettes a day and drinks one beer a day.
She has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of IV drug use and alcohol use, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She currently smokes one to two cigarettes a day and drinks one beer a day.
Review of systems: The patient reports feeling tired at the end of the day, despite getting enough sleep, but attributes this to work-related stress.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
Heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
Lung exam is clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Abdominal exam is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly.
Musculoskeletal exam is normal with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: The patient's recent blood work shows a positive HCV antibody test and an elevated AST of 39, but normal ALT, albumin, and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has been diagnosed with hepatitis C, a viral infection affecting the liver.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; active.
Plan:
I prescribed a liver ultrasound to determine the severity of liver disease and a confirmatory test to check for HCV RNA and genotype.
I will also recommend that the patient's family members undergo screening for HCV.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in about three weeks to discuss further management and treatment options.
